thesis: Decentralized infrastructure for sovereign data governance — using regulated behavioral health as the deliberate proving ground for architectures that scale across complex domains.
footer_descriptor: Decentralized infrastructure for sovereign data governance

layers.name: Financial
layers.description: Live market integration, ISO 20022 cohort (XRP, XLM, HBAR, ALGO, ADA), XRPL payment rails, HNT participation tokenomics

layers.name: Identity
layers.description: EIP-191 wallet-gated authentication, Brave Wallet primary, SovereignSession wallet-native video, no PHI held by design

layers.name: Governance
layers.description: SovereignLedger session attestation, 17-agent autonomous pipeline, on-chain provenance

layers.name: Compliance
layers.description: Operates outside HIPAA scope by holding no PHI — the regulation does not apply when the system never holds the protected data

layers.name: Therapeutic
layers.description: 45 games, SovereignSession peer-to-peer video, AlchemistForge shadow integration, Sovereign Guide framework

layers.name: Research
layers.description: DEng Applied Project for ASU — the deployed system IS the research contribution

boscovic_alignment: Independent architecture arriving at convergent framing through different cryptographic primitives (public Ethereum + EIP-191 vs. permissioned Hyperledger Fabric + proxy re-encryption). Designed by a credentialed practitioner who lived both sides of the system failure.

deng.school: Arizona State University, Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering
deng.prerequisite: BS MIS from CSU Global confers July 2026 (GPA 3.94)
